verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 is a canonical ER-resident, multi-pass (6TM) Ca²⁺ channel whose transmembrane domain is embedded in the ER membrane with both its large N-terminal IP3-binding domain and C-terminal cytoplasmic domain facing the cytosol—not the extracellular space. All primary literature consistently places ITPR3 in the ER (and subdomains such as MAM and sub-apical ER). Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced—no documented outer-leaflet display in cancer, cell death, or activation states for ITPR3 specifically; (2) tissue_restricted_surface—no narrow germline/developmental lineage PM display reported; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis—ITPR3 is not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization—no documented PM minority pool via surface biotinylation or flow cytometry on intact cells; (5) stable_surface_attachment—no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to PM reported. One commercial vendor claims PM expression at nodes of Ranvier, but this is contradicted by the full body of peer-reviewed literature and is unreliable. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs identified.
